pages.title: Markdown To HTML Preview Chain
pages.summary: Explains how handbook Markdown, framework references, and HTML hover previews stay separated from project Conclusion Evidence.

pages.title: Auto-Iterate Model
pages.summary: Explains how the WF10 auto-iterate controller fits into the workflow.

pages.title: Evidence And Approval Model
pages.summary: Separates Conclusion Evidence, Gate Evidence, Evidence Chains, and approvals.

pages.title: Hooks And Permissions Model
pages.summary: Explains why Harness hooks block missing reads, out-of-scope writes, and missing ledgers.

pages.title: Build And Validate Layer
pages.summary: How WF8-WF9 implement and validate bounded code slices.

pages.title: Contract And Plan Layer
pages.summary: How WF5-WF7 establish baseline, boundaries, architecture, and slices.

pages.title: Explore Layer
pages.summary: How WF0-WF4 move from idea to data facts.

pages.title: Iterate And Release Layer
pages.summary: How WF10-WF12 run iterations, final experiment, and release.

pages.title: Skill Reference
pages.summary: Entry point for detailed Skill Contract pages.

pages.title: Workflow Layers
pages.summary: Explains the Intent -> Stage -> Skill -> Gate model.

pages.title: Workflow Terms
pages.summary: Clickable term targets for handbook references.

pages.title: auto-iterate-goal
pages.summary: Generate or validate the auto-iterate goal file before launching WF10 auto-iterate

pages.title: baseline-repro
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for WF5 baseline reproduction. Use when the user wants baseline adaptation, reproduction tracking, and `docs/Baseline_Report.md` following the original workflow contract.

pages.title: build-plan
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for WF7 implementation planning. Use after WF6 architecture design when the user wants `docs/Implementation_Roadmap.md`, `project_map.json`, and `docs/20_facts/Codebase_Map.md` built from the technical spec, baseline evidence, templates, and schemas.

pages.title: code-debug
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for post-WF8 repository implementation code modification and debugging. Use for planned iteration changes, bug fixes, or tightly scoped performance edits under src, scripts, configs, project_map, or Codebase_Map. Do not use for Codex hooks, skill contracts, skill routing, or permission policy; use harness-maintenance for those.

pages.title: code-expert
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for WF8 first-pass code generation. Use when the user wants implementation generated directly from `project_map.json`, `docs/20_facts/Codebase_Map.md`, the roadmap, and the original Claude skill contract.

pages.title: code-review
pages.summary: Review code, docs-supporting code, and git diffs with line-referenced findings, git metadata, Codex review, optional external model cross-check, and a reconciled report.

pages.title: data-prep
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for WF4 data engineering. Use when the user wants dataset analysis, subset strategy selection, and `docs/Dataset_Stats.md` produced according to the original workflow.

pages.title: deep-check
pages.summary: Codex design-review gate for WF6 architecture decisions. Use when the user wants a skeptical Go/No-Go review of the technical spec before implementation planning or heavy implementation starts.

pages.title: doc-compiler
pages.summary: Compile current project documents from explicit evidence chains. Use when refreshing contract, fact, protocol, or release docs that need auditable evidence.

pages.title: docs-site
pages.summary: Render source Markdown project docs into human-readable HTML under docs/_site, with Evidence Chain hover previews from docs/_views/evidence_preview_index.json. Use after stable Markdown docs are finalized, before human review or handoff, or when explicitly rebuilding the human docs site.

pages.title: env-setup
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for environment creation and refresh. Use when the user wants the environment detected, created, or synchronized into the legacy `CLAUDE.md` format.

pages.title: evaluate
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for experiment analysis and decision-making. Use when the user wants metrics interpreted, a stage or iteration report written, and a NEXT_ROUND, DEBUG, CONTINUE, PIVOT, or ABORT recommendation.

pages.title: final-exp
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for WF11 final experiment planning. Use when the user wants ablations, robustness tests, cross-dataset evaluation, and compute budgeting organized according to the original template.

pages.title: harness-maintenance
pages.summary: Maintain Harness framework guardrails: Codex hooks, evidence tooling guardrails, skill contracts, skill routing/triggers, permission policy docs, schema/tests, bootstrap templates, and .agents/.claude guidance alignment. Use when modifying tooling/codex_hooks, tooling/evidence guardrails, schemas/skill_contracts.json, schemas/skill_contracts.schema.json, .agents/skills, .agents/references, .claude/Workflow_Guide.md, .claude/skills, .claude/rules, .claude/shared, templates, hook detection, hook trust/status, schema validation, or permission boundaries.

pages.title: idea-debate
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for WF2 idea debate. Use after WF1 feasibility to stress-test candidate research directions before WF3 refine-idea and before any architecture design.

pages.title: init-project
pages.summary: WF0/bootstrap wrapper for staged `CLAUDE.md` generation and updates. Use when the user wants the compact project snapshot initialized or refreshed while preserving the original staged template behavior.

pages.title: iterate
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for WF10 structured iteration. Use when the user wants to run `plan`, `code`, `run`, `eval`, `ablate`, `status`, or `log` while preserving the original iteration schema and workflow logic.

pages.title: orchestrator
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for the canonical WF orchestrator. Use when the user wants project initialization, stage status, gate checks, rollback, or decision logging around `PROJECT_STATE.json`.

pages.title: protocol-compiler
pages.summary: Compile Dynamic Research Protocol drafts from current evidence tables without using pre-baked research profiles.

pages.title: protocol-drift-check
pages.summary: Check whether dynamic research protocol drafts are stale before baseline, iteration, final experiment, or release gates.

pages.title: refine-arch
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for WF6 architecture design. Use after WF4 data preparation and WF5 baseline reproduction to convert the refined idea, dataset facts, baseline evidence, and evaluation contract into a technical spec and MVP architecture.

pages.title: refine-idea
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for WF3 idea refinement. Use after WF1 survey and WF2 idea debate to turn the selected direction into a feasible research idea, task framing, success criteria, baseline requirements, and protocol assumptions without designing the architecture.

pages.title: release
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for WF12 release and submission packaging. Use when the user wants validation, packaging, or submission preparation according to the original workflow.

pages.title: review-packet
pages.summary: Build concise human review packets for dynamic-context contracts, protocol readiness, and release gates.

pages.title: survey-idea
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for WF1 idea survey and feasibility analysis. Use when the user wants literature-backed validation of a new research idea and a `docs/Feasibility_Report.md` outcome.

pages.title: validate-run
pages.summary: Codex wrapper for WF9 validation. Use when the user wants the training chain reviewed and smoke-tested before entering WF10.

pages.title: WF0 Init
pages.summary: Initialize or refresh the workspace guidance and workflow state.

pages.title: WF10 Iterate
pages.summary: Run the Ralph-style loop: plan, code, run, evaluate, and decide the next round.

pages.title: WF11 Final Exp
pages.summary: Run final experiment checks against approved contracts and claim boundaries.

pages.title: WF12 Release
pages.summary: Prepare release artifacts while keeping claims inside the approved boundary.

pages.title: WF1 Survey Idea
pages.summary: Collect early Conclusion Evidence and decide whether the idea is worth pursuing.

pages.title: WF2 Idea Debate
pages.summary: Compare candidate directions and choose the strongest research path.

pages.title: WF3 Refine Idea
pages.summary: Turn the selected idea into a tighter research question and execution target.

pages.title: WF4 Data Prep
pages.summary: Make data facts explicit before baseline or architecture work starts.

pages.title: WF5 Baseline Repro
pages.summary: Reproduce or establish a baseline and prepare approval-facing contracts.

pages.title: WF6 Refine Arch
pages.summary: Refine the technical architecture within approved boundaries.

pages.title: WF7 Build Plan
pages.summary: Convert the architecture into bounded implementation slices.

pages.title: WF8 Code Expert
pages.summary: Implement one bounded code slice under the current plan.

pages.title: WF9 Validate Run
pages.summary: Validate the implementation before structured iteration.
